Tissue microarrays (TMA)

Large number of sample cores from specimen tissues
Immunohistochemistry or fluorescent in-situ hybridization
Signal is read through imaging

Lin, J.R. et al, Multiplexed 3D atlas of state transitions and immune interaction in 
colorectal cancer. Cell. 2023 Jan 19;186(2):363-381.e19.

Bost, P et al., Optimizing multiplexed imaging experimental design through tissue 
spatial segregation estimation. Nat Methods. 2023 Mar;20(3):418-423.

The required width of imaged Fields of Vision (FoV) depends on the spatial features of the tissue.
 - Studied cells had correlation lengths between 80-400 µm. 
 - TMA cores often have a radius of 300-500 µm.
Are TMAs a reliable method for studying spatial organizations and abundances of tumor cells?

05/11/2024

Faculty of Science Thesis project / Antti Toivanen

9

Things of note

05/11/2024

Faculty of Science Thesis project / Antti Toivanen

10

Quantification of cell types and states associated with prognostically significant tumor features in colorectal cancer (CRC).

Examined data from 93 CRC tumors
17 cases with Whole-Slide Imaging (WSI) 
92 with Tissue Microarrays (TMA)
Combined 40 sections from one tumor sample into a 3D model, with GeoMx transcriptomics

05/11/2024

Faculty of Science Thesis project / Antti Toivanen

11

Multiplexed 3D atlas of state transitions and immune interaction in colorectal cancer

Findings

Tumor structures often perceived in 2D are in fact connected 3D
Large-scale morphological and molecular gradients
Accurate assessment of tumor structures requires WSI instead of TMA

05/11/2024

Faculty of Science Thesis project / Antti Toivanen

12

Lin, J.R. et al, Multiplexed 3D atlas of state transitions and immune interaction in 
colorectal cancer. Cell. 2023 Jan 19;186(2):363-381.e19.

Faculty of Science Thesis project / Antti Toivanen

13

Development of a statistical imaging framework for identifying all cell phenotypes in a tissue
Model allows estimating optimal imaging study parameters

Visium spatial RNA-transcriptomic data from 22 datasets / 12 tissues.
Random sampling of square Fields of Vision (FoV) used to model relationship between number of samples and captured cell types.
Validated on Imaging Mass Cytometry (IMC) data from multiple tissue types.

Quantifying the number of different cell phenotypes in a tissue.
Easier in non-segregated tissues. 
Preference for many small samples in segregated tissues.

Bost, P et al., Optimizing multiplexed imaging experimental design through tissue 
spatial segregation estimation. Nat Methods. 2023 Mar;20(3):418-423.

What are tma not capable of?

Quantification of cell type proportions in highly spatially segregated tissues.


Characterization of large tissue features
